source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
artist: Unknown photographer (Southeast Alaska gelatin silver postcard, 1900–1940)
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Canoe, Ketchican [Ketchikan], Alaska
description: Historic gelatin silver postcard showing a traditional Tlingit canoe on calm Ketchikan waters with the dense Tongass rainforest wall rising directly from the shoreline behind it. Tlingit seagoing canoes were the primary form of travel along Southeast Alaska's fjords and inlets for thousands of years before motorized vessels, and they were especially suited to the protected waterways of what is now Misty Fjords National Monument.
alt: Historic sepia postcard of a traditional Tlingit seagoing canoe with a paddler on Ketchikan waters, Tongass rainforest behind
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from tile.loc.gov and visually confirmed
